scores.brief_feedback: Comprehensive coverage of all consolidation dimensions with excellent examples (Patel's integration, FYPs, IITs, NAM). Exceeds word limit by 50% but maintains strong factual accuracy and structure.

Well-articulated on French Revolution's enduring relevance with good connection to contemporary issues. References UDHR 1948 and Indian Constitution. Could have provided more concrete modern examples; exceeds word limit by 27%.

Excellent geographical analysis of offshore vs. onshore oil reserves with specific field examples (Persian Gulf, Campos Basin) and technical accuracy. Exceeds word limit by 40%; strong depth on extraction technologies and environmental implications.

Integrates AI, drones, GIS, and RS effectively with practical examples (SVAMITVA, PM Gati Shakti, MGNREGA). Strong systems-thinking on technology convergence. Exceeds word limit by 41%; policy references (National Geospatial Policy 2022, Drone Rules 2021) well-placed.

Strong explanation of plate tectonics and continental change via divergent/convergent/transform boundaries. Excellent examples (East African Rift, Atlantic spreading, Himalayas, San Andreas). Exceeds word limit by 32%; demonstrates solid understanding of Wilson Cycle.

Well-articulated on Ganga Basin population dynamics, connecting topography, alluvial soils, and water availability. Specific density figures (Bihar 1,106/sq.km, WB 1,028) accurate. Addresses resource stress (groundwater depletion). Exceeds word limit by 29%.

Good analysis of fast food growth paradox with specific data (QSR CAGR 15%, platforms: Swiggy/Zomato). Discusses localization, affordability, and industry 'healthwashing.' Policy recommendations (FSSAI, nutritional labeling) appropriate. Exceeds word limit by 16%.

Balanced examination of sustainability vs. poverty tension with specific cases (Wildlife Act displacement, coal mining regions, groundwater regulations). Presents counter-argument on environment-poverty linkage and 'Just Transition' concept. Exceeds word limit by 25%.

Nuanced analysis transcending simple displacement/rehabilitation dichotomy. Strong references (Xaxa Committee, Narmada, Niyamgiri, FRA 2006, PESA) and discussion of 'Integration without Assimilation.' Exceeds word limit by 38%; sophisticated argument on agency and empowerment.

Accurate on RPA Section 123 corrupt practices and Lok Prahari case. Good distinction between asset increase vs. concealment as undue influence. Exceeds word limit by 52%; stronger focus on presentation constraint needed.

Good coverage of tribunal advantages (specialization, de-clogging courts) and 2021 Reforms Act concerns. Cites Madras Bar Association case. Recommendations on National Tribunals Commission appropriate. Exceeds word limit by 37%.

Accurate comparison of presidential pardon powers (Article 72 India vs. Article II USA). Cites Epuru Sudhakar case and explains preemptive pardons via Ford-Nixon example. Exceeds word limit by 29%; could elaborate on constitutional philosophical differences.

Accurate on J&K Assembly post-2019: 114 seats, exceptions on Public Order/Police, LG discretion. Cites Ranjana Desai Delimitation and discusses 'asymmetric federalism.' Exceeds word limit by 31%; could provide more on legislative-executive dynamics.

Good coverage of AG's role via Article 76, parliamentary privileges (Article 88), immunity (Article 105), and limitations. Distinguishes India's AG from US Cabinet position. Exceeds word limit by 43%; succinct but accurate.

Strong on women's social capital (SHGs, DAY-NRLM, Lakhpati Didi, Kudumbashree). References Grameen Bank, Article 243D reservations, and SDG 5. Exceeds word limit by 17%; well-connected to empowerment outcomes.

Good identification of e-governance tech bias over UX design. Cites WCAG gaps, vernacular support gaps, and compares to GOV.UK/Estonia models. Recommends 2nd ARC ideas. Exceeds word limit by 37%; relevant contemporary examples.

Balanced analysis of CSOs: acknowledges anti-State perception but argues for collaborative governance model. Cites Akshaya Patra, MKSS/RTI movement, international precedent. Exceeds word limit by 15%; nuanced perspective on regulation.

Well-explained India-Africa digital partnership via Kampala Principles, MOSIP adoption, ITEC, PAENP, e-VBAB. Distinguishes from neo-colonial models. Exceeds word limit by 29%; good institutional detail.

Good analysis of post-Cold War sovereign nationalism trends (protectionism, supply chain resilience, geopolitical assertiveness). Cites Atmanirbhar Bharat, SCRI, Brexit. Exceeds word limit by 26%; connects to India's strategic autonomy.

Sophisticated analysis of Constitutional Morality balancing judicial independence and accountability. Cites NCT Delhi case, Bangalore Principles, Subhash Chandra Agarwal RTI case. Discusses institutional mechanisms (Collegium, impeachment, 2nd ARC). Exceeds word limit by 29%.
